summary: A clear visual indicator in the spell description or on the target's health bar that dynamically shows if the target is currently valid for the spell's HP threshold.
game_context: Cast the Sleep spell successfully to neutralize a target.
evidence: UI displays 'Too many Hit Points' error when hovering over target
Scene: Campsite combat, player attempting to use the 'Sleep' spell on a companion.
Quote: I can't put you to sleep, I tried.

summary: A more prominent explanation or tooltip popup describing the 'Surprised' condition the first time it is triggered, explaining that it consumes the first turn.
game_context: Engage in combat immediately upon the encounter starting.
evidence: Surprised status icon visible on portraits
Scene: Blighted Village basement, surprise encounter with Robust Guardian.
Quote: I'm surprised. Help me, I'm surprised.

summary: Better visual signposting of safe zones or a clearer way to see trap 'cycles' to allow for timing-based movement.
game_context: Exit the room safely after looting the cocoon.
evidence: Character standing still in the center of repeating fire blasts
Camera rotation searching for an exit
Scene: Small side room in the cellar with active fire traps.
Quote: Except I am stuck in here.

summary: Clearer feedback or environmental storytelling if the room contains no further significant loot or secrets, preventing the player from feeling they've missed something.
game_context: Loot relevant items and move on to the next objective.
evidence: Scene: Blighted Village basement, post-combat exploration.
Quote: What do you want me to do in here?
Quote: How they expect me to loot

summary: Faster animation options or 'simultaneous turns' for clusters of identical minor enemies to maintain engagement during large-scale encounters.
game_context: Follow the sequence of combat actions and movement from NPCs.
evidence: waiting for NPC turns to complete
Scene: Turn-based combat encounter in the Sunlit Wetlands with multiple Mephits.
Quote: Cannot watch them all move three inches, please, can I not, come on.

summary: A more distinct visual highlight or a glint on the quest-specific interactable object (the cleft) when the player is in close proximity.
game_context: Locate the 'cleft' in the tree to progress the 'Investigate Kagha' quest line.
evidence: running back and forth across the wooden bridge
Scene: Exploring the river area and the large tree at the Druid Sanctuary.
Quote: Which way are we going?

summary: A recurring tooltip or loading screen hint suggesting manual saves before entering high-danger zones like the Sanctuary.
game_context: Utilize manual quicksaves frequently to minimize progress loss in a high-lethality game.
evidence: Scene: The 'Game Over' screen after the party is defeated by Kagha.

summary: Clearer feedback on why certain environmental units (the rats) are absent from the initiative order compared to the previous attempt.
game_context: Understand the conditions for enemy participation in the battle.
evidence: panning the camera over the arena
inspecting empty spaces where NPCs previously stood
Scene: Inside the sanctuary during the re-initiated combat encounter with the druids.
Quote: I think it's cause you scared off the rats so they're not even a part of this.

summary: Subtle visual signposting (like wear-and-tear textures or a slight path highlight) to differentiate climbable/walkable natural rock geometry from decorative terrain.
game_context: Follow the group by identifying the natural rock staircase path.
evidence: Standing still on a ledge while teammates are out of frame
Scene: On the rocky cliffs overlooking the Emerald Grove waterfall path.
Quote: Quentin I'm very lost and afraid.
Quote: How was I supposed to know this was a staircase?

summary: Better off-screen teammate indicators that account for significant vertical distance, or a more persistent camera tracking option for group movement.
game_context: Maintain group cohesion while navigating vertical architecture.
evidence: Rapid camera rotation searching for player icons
Scene: The wooden stairs and platforms of the Risen Road building.
Quote: Where'd you just go dude?
Quote: Where'd you just fucking go?
Quote: Stop teleporting away dude. Stop fucking teleporting away.

summary: A specific UI tag or visual border on items that have been flagged or discussed as Gale-eligible to prevent accidental equipping in a co-op inventory.
game_context: Distinguish between equipment for party use and magical artifacts designated for consumption by Gale.
evidence: Scene: On the wooden deck, managing inventory items meant for Gale's quest line.
Quote: Are you wearing the helmet that I gave you?
Quote: You're supposed to feed Gale.
Quote: Give me back that helmet mf.

summary: Better camera collision or transparency for foreground walls in tight interiors to prevent the player from perceiving a soft-lock.
game_context: Continue looting environmental containers in the basement area.
evidence: Camera clipping through a stone wall
Player character completely obscured from view
Scene: Inside the basement of the building. The player character is standing in a corner or behind a wall while looting vases.
Quote: I'm getting shut in here. Hello?
